source_context.focus: B2B executive coaches and executive search firms serving strong and medium fit senior executives.
source_context.core_gtm_principle: Do not sell job search. Sell executive readiness, signal intelligence, and better conversations.
source_context.recommended_starting_channel: Executive coaches first; boutique retained IT executive search firms second.

executive_readiness_partner_council.purpose: Evaluate how Starting Monday can partner with executive coaches who serve senior technology leaders.
executive_readiness_partner_council.core_question: Would executive coaches see Starting Monday as a client accelerator, referral asset, differentiation tool, or threat?
evaluates: how Starting Monday helps coaches deliver better outcomes
what offer coaches would actually share with clients
how to position Starting Monday without sounding like AI replacing coaching

best_customer_fit.persona: VP Technology / VP IT moving toward CIO

subgroups.name: Elite C-Suite Behavior Change Coaches
subgroups.role: Test whether Starting Monday helps executives become more effective, coachable, and self-aware.

subgroups.name: Career Transition / Identity Coaches
subgroups.role: Test whether Starting Monday helps executives move into a new leadership identity.

subgroups.name: Technology Executive Coaches
subgroups.role: Test fit with CIO, CTO, CISO, and CDO clients specifically.

subgroups.name: Coaching Business Model / Channel Advisors
subgroups.role: Test whether coaches would actually refer, resell, bundle, or co-brand Starting Monday.

members.name: Marshall Goldsmith
members.role: Executive behavior change and C-suite coaching
first_principles: Successful executives can outgrow the behaviors that made them successful.
Measurable behavior change matters more than self-image.
Feedback and follow-through are the foundation of executive growth.
Coachability predicts future leadership success.
starting_monday_lens: Does the product help executives see their blind spots?
Does it make executives more coachable?
Can it create structured prep that improves a coach's work?
Would a coach trust this as a complement rather than replacement?
members.likely_critique: Starting Monday must not present itself as an AI coach replacement. It should position as career intelligence plus preparation infrastructure that makes coaching more effective.

members.name: Herminia Ibarra
members.role: Career transition, leadership identity, and executive reinvention
first_principles: Career transition is not purely analytical; it is identity work.
Executives often act into a new identity before they fully believe it.
Networks shape opportunity.
Reinvention happens through experiments, not just planning.
starting_monday_lens: Does the product help executives become the next version of themselves?
Does it help them experiment with roles, narratives, and networks?
Does it reduce fear during transition?
Does it support pre-search exploration before public movement?
members.likely_critique: If Starting Monday feels too mechanical, it will miss the emotional and identity-based reality of executive transition.

members.name: Michael Bungay Stanier
members.role: Practical coaching conversations and curiosity-led leadership
first_principles: Ask more, tell less.
Stay curious longer.
Coaching must be practical enough to use repeatedly.
Good questions create better self-discovery than advice dumps.
starting_monday_lens: Does Starting Monday ask better questions?
Does it produce useful reflection, or just recommendations?
Can coaches use its outputs to improve sessions?
Can it help clients arrive prepared?
members.likely_critique: The product should avoid becoming a do-this machine. It should create better coaching conversations.

members.name: Nathalie Heynderickx
first_principles: Technology executives need sustainable performance, not just career acceleration.
CIOs and senior IT leaders face emotional, political, and strategic pressures.
Community and reflection are part of leadership growth.
Burnout can distort career decisions.
starting_monday_lens: Does Starting Monday understand CIO psychology?
Does it help technology leaders avoid reactive moves?
Does it support sustainable leadership and career clarity?
Could CIO coaches use it as client intelligence infrastructure?
members.likely_critique: Starting Monday must feel emotionally intelligent and human, not just analytically impressive.

members.name: Seth Godin
members.role: Trust, permission, tribes, smallest viable audience
first_principles: Permission beats interruption.
Smallest viable audience beats generic scale.
People like us do things like this.
Remarkable products earn conversations.
starting_monday_lens: Which coach niche is the smallest viable audience?
What would make coaches proud to recommend Starting Monday?
How does Starting Monday earn permission from coaches?
What story makes this feel valuable, not threatening?
members.likely_critique: Do not pitch AI helps your clients find jobs. Pitch your clients arrive more prepared, more informed, and more confident.

members.name: Robert Cialdini
members.role: Ethical persuasion, authority, social proof, trust signals
first_principles: Trust must precede ask.
Authority and social proof reduce perceived risk.
Reciprocity and commitment can support adoption.
Pre-suasion shapes what prospects notice and value.
starting_monday_lens: What proof would make coaches trust the product?
What authority signals are missing?
Should coaches receive a sample client prep brief?
How can Starting Monday ethically earn referrals?
members.likely_critique: Coach-channel conversion will fail without proof, authority, and low-risk trial mechanics.

recommended_positioning.do_not_say: We help your clients find jobs.
recommended_positioning.say: We help your senior technology clients monitor opportunity signals, clarify their executive narrative, and arrive to coaching/search conversations better prepared.
recommended_positioning.core_offer: Give your clients a private executive opportunity intelligence layer between coaching sessions.

pilot_offer.name: Coach Partner Preview
components: optional referral fee or partner offer

search_intelligence_partner_council.purpose: Evaluate how Starting Monday can partner with boutique and retained executive search firms serving senior technology executives.
search_intelligence_partner_council.core_question: Would search firms see Starting Monday as candidate-prep infrastructure, sourcing intelligence, market signal tooling, referral partner, or competitive threat?
evaluates: whether Starting Monday should sell to search firms now or later

subgroups.name: Global Retained Search Leaders
subgroups.role: Useful for category credibility and enterprise search realism.

subgroups.name: PE / Transformation Search Specialists
subgroups.role: High value because they care about organizational signals, transformation mandates, and timing.

subgroups.name: Search Workflow / Candidate Intelligence Advisors
subgroups.role: Evaluate whether Starting Monday helps or interferes with search workflow.

members.name: Steve Kendrick
members.role: Senior IT executive search and CIO/CTO retained search
first_principles: Executive fit is relationship- and context-dependent.
CIO/CTO searches require credibility and trust.
Role timing often appears before public job activity.
Search firms own sensitive context that tools usually miss.
starting_monday_lens: Would a boutique IT search firm refer candidates to Starting Monday?
Would it see Starting Monday as a threat?
Does the scanner produce signals a search consultant would respect?
Can Starting Monday improve candidate readiness before search engagement?
members.likely_critique: If positioned as a search replacement, search firms will resist. If positioned as executive readiness plus market intelligence, they may engage.

members.name: Craig Stephenson
members.role: CIO/CTO search, digital officer recruiting, leadership development
first_principles: Technology officer search is about leadership fit, not keyword matching.
Enterprise search requires process rigor and confidentiality.
CIO/CTO readiness includes business transformation ability.
Leadership development and succession are part of the search ecosystem.
starting_monday_lens: Could Starting Monday become a pre-search readiness layer?
Does fit modeling understand CIO/CTO leadership complexity?
Would large search firms trust its intelligence?
Does it help executives prepare for search-firm conversations?
members.likely_critique: Starting Monday needs proof that its intelligence is credible, explainable, and additive to search professionals.

members.name: Katie Graham Shannon
members.role: Digital, technology, and AI officer search
first_principles: Modern technology leaders must drive measurable business impact.
AI, digital, security, engineering, and customer experience leadership increasingly overlap.
Executive fit depends on transformation context.
PE and VC environments require different leadership patterns.
starting_monday_lens: Does Starting Monday detect companies moving toward transformation needs?
Does it understand AI/digital officer role evolution?
Can it identify fit between executive background and transformation mandate?
Could it support PE-backed executive readiness?
members.likely_critique: The product must avoid static role taxonomies. Technology leadership roles are changing too quickly.

members.name: Eric Sigurdson
members.role: CIO practice and technology transformation executive search
first_principles: CIO fit balances leadership, culture, transformation history, and business context.
Technology leaders are increasingly enterprise strategists.
Reference networks and reputation matter.
Search is about fit under real organizational pressure.
starting_monday_lens: Does Starting Monday understand cultural and transformation fit?
Does the executive fit model go beyond title/resume matching?
Can it surface market context search firms care about?
Can it help executives become more findable and better prepared?
members.likely_critique: Starting Monday needs outcome proof: did intelligence lead to better conversations, faster introductions, or better-fit opportunities?

members.name: Chris Voss
members.role: Negotiation, discovery, objection handling, trust-sensitive conversations
first_principles: Tactical empathy reveals hidden resistance.
No can be the start of the real conversation.
Calibrated questions uncover true objections.
Labels reduce defensiveness.
starting_monday_lens: What are search firms afraid Starting Monday will become?
How should we label threat concerns?
What questions reveal partnerability?
How do we negotiate pilot terms without sounding desperate?
members.likely_critique: Do not pitch search firms. Start by understanding what they protect: trust, client control, candidate quality, confidentiality, and process authority.

members.name: Bob Moore
first_principles: Partnerships should create shared pipeline or shared intelligence.
Partner overlap reveals hidden demand.
Ecosystems can create more trust than direct selling.
BD must be measurable.
starting_monday_lens: Which search firms share the same executive audience?
Which partners have complementary incentives?
What does a measurable pilot look like?
Which search-firm partnerships create data/network advantages?
members.likely_critique: Do not collect logos. Build partner motions with clear economics, referrals, or intelligence benefits.

recommended_positioning.do_not_say: We help executives find jobs before you do.
recommended_positioning.say: We help executives arrive more prepared, better positioned, and more informed — which can improve the quality of search conversations.
recommended_positioning.core_offer: A private executive readiness and market signal layer that improves search conversations.

pilot_offer.name: Search Intelligence Partner Pilot
components: feedback session on signal quality and fit model credibility

week_1_build_channel_assets.message: Help senior technology clients become opportunity-ready before the call comes.
A private executive readiness and market signal layer that improves search conversations.

week_2_outreach_test.goal: 8-10 conversations focused on learning, not sales.
week_2_outreach_test.primary_question: Would this help or threaten your work with senior technology executives?

decision_criteria.threshold: low/medium for coaches; no higher than medium for search

contact_enrichment_and_fit_rubric_v1_1.purpose: Operationalize Apollo-assisted contact expansion and auto-segment contacts into strong and medium fit tiers with deterministic rules.

fit_segmentation_rules.explicit_override: If fit_tier is present in source row and is strong or medium, use it.

target_slate_join.rule: If the company-role pair exists in us-senior-executive-target-slate.csv, assign that fit_tier directly.

confidence_and_conflict_policy.field_normalization: Treat confidence and email_confidence as equivalent inputs.
confidence_and_conflict_policy.conflict_rule: When two records conflict for the same person, keep the higher-precedence source and latest verification date.

dedupe_and_refresh.dedupe_rule: Prefer strong over medium; if tie, prefer higher email confidence; if tie, prefer higher source precedence.
